ogTitle: Warkworth vs Warners Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Warkworth and Warners Bay.
heading: Warkworth vs Warners Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Warkworth, New South Wales and Warners Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Warners Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Warkworth (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Warkworth or Warners Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Warkworth has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Warners Bay scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Warkworth scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Warners Bay scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Warkworth or Warners Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Warners Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
